label: 💬 llm-analyzer

validate

label: 🤖 ml-inference

name: ml-inference — ONNX model test-models/times_two.onnx mounted at /data/models
info: The model computes output = input * 2. Input [1,2,3,4] -> prediction [2,4,6,8]. Needs the Debian image (onnxruntime-node) — see docker-compose.yml.

validate

label: 🎓 trained: defect detected

label: 🌍 pretrained: SqueezeNet
info: A real pretrained ImageNet classifier (ONNX Model Zoo) on a real photo of a dog. Full chain incl. ImageNet normalization.

name: image-preprocess (imagenet) → squeezenet.onnx (pretrained) → vision-annotator (classification)
info: View: GET /preview?test=pretrained: SqueezeNet

name: gen
func:
const SEQ = [{"__b64":""}];
const EXPECT = {"annotations.mode":"classification","annotations.topClass":258,"annotations.topLabel":"Samoyed","annotations.topScore":{"min":0.5,"max":1}};
const TEST = "pretrained: SqueezeNet classify";
for (let i = 0; i < SEQ.length; i++) {
    const el = SEQ[i];
    let m;
    if (el && typeof el === 'object' && !Array.isArray(el) && el.__b64) {
        m = { payload: Buffer.from(el.__b64, 'base64') };
    } else if (el && typeof el === 'object' && !Array.isArray(el) && el.__msg) {
        m = Object.assign({}, el); delete m.__msg;
    } else {
        m = { payload: el };
    }
    if (i === SEQ.length - 1 && EXPECT) { m.test = TEST; m.expect = EXPECT; }
    node.send(m);
}
return null;

func: msg.test = "pretrained: SqueezeNet classify"; return msg;

label: 🌍 pretrained: YOLOv10
info: A real pretrained YOLOv10n detector (NMS-free, COCO) on a real photo. Boxes drawn back on the image.

name: image-preprocess (640, 0..1) → yolov10n.onnx → vision-annotator (boxes xyxy, COCO)
info: View: GET /preview?test=pretrained: YOLOv10

name: gen
func:
const SEQ = [{"__b64":""}];
const EXPECT = {"annotations.mode":"boxes","annotations.count":{"min":1,"max":300},"annotations.classesPresent":[16]};
const TEST = "pretrained: YOLOv10 detect";
for (let i = 0; i < SEQ.length; i++) {
    const el = SEQ[i];
    let m;
    if (el && typeof el === 'object' && !Array.isArray(el) && el.__b64) {
        m = { payload: Buffer.from(el.__b64, 'base64') };
    } else if (el && typeof el === 'object' && !Array.isArray(el) && el.__msg) {
        m = Object.assign({}, el); delete m.__msg;
    } else {
        m = { payload: el };
    }
    if (i === SEQ.length - 1 && EXPECT) { m.test = TEST; m.expect = EXPECT; }
    node.send(m);
}
return null;

    } else if (el && typeof el === 'object' && !Array.isArray(el) && el.__msg) {
        m = Object.assign({}, el); delete m.__msg;
    } else {
        m = { payload: el };
    }
    if (i === SEQ.length - 1 && EXPECT) { m.test = TEST; m.expect = EXPECT; }
    node.send(m);
}
return null;

name: function
tag
func: msg.test = "pretrained: Pose keypoints"; return msg;

name: validate

label: 🔬 derived: anomaly (from seg)
info: The defect model's class-1 score field rendered as an anomaly heatmap with thresholded regions.

name: gen
function
func: const f=msg.prediction; const s=msg.mlInference.outputShape; const H=s[2],W=s[3]; msg.prediction=f.slice(H*W,2*H*W); msg.mlInference.outputShape=[1,1,H,W]; return msg;

name: tag
func: msg.test = "derived: anomaly from seg"; return msg;

name: validate

label: 🔬 derived: instances (from seg)
info: The defect mask as one instance (colour + bbox + area) via instance-segmentation rendering.

name: gen
function
func:
const f = msg.prediction; const s = msg.mlInference.outputShape; const H = s[2], W = s[3]; const fg = (x,y)=>f[H*W+y*W+x] > f[y*W+x];
const bin=new Array(H*W); for(let y=0;y<H;y++)for(let x=0;x<W;x++)bin[y*W+x]=fg(x,y)?1:0; msg.prediction=bin; msg.mlInference.outputShape=[1,1,H,W]; return msg;

name: tag
func: msg.test = "derived: instances from seg"; return msg;

name: validate

label: 🔬 derived: polygons (from seg)
info: The defect mask outlined as a polygon (convex hull) — area + perimeter measured.

name: gen

info: The json-source simulates JSON sensor records; multi-value-processor aggregates them. Proves arbitrary JSON data can be simulated for the object/record nodes.

label: 🏭 source waveform → signal-analyzer
info: The condition-monitoring-source emits a real vibration time-signal (imbalance fault); signal-analyzer's FFT recovers the fault frequency at the shaft speed (rpm 1500 → 25 Hz).

name: condition-monitoring-source (waveform, imbalance) → signal-analyzer (FFT) → dominant freq ≈ 25 Hz
info: Proves the simulator drives spectral analysis, not just the trend nodes.
